---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Julian Mittmann-Goetsch"
date: "2025-04-28"
output: html_document
---
# Step 2: DESeq2 Analysis
Following: https://lashlock.github.io/compbio/R_presentation.html 
## Step 2a: Intitialise DESeq 2 analysis
```{r}
# Prepare the count data, excluding MOCK samples and neg and pos controls

## IMPORTANT: CUrrently we add a count of 1 to each sample, to avoid errors with log functions later on, we could set this to an even smaller number or do some other workaround
countData <- tab_rare  %>%
  select(-tail(names(tab_rare), 6), -c("X136", "X137", "X138", "X139")) %>%
  #rownames_to_column(var = "ASV") %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ . + 1))

# Prepare the metaData for the dds matrix, add Information on Layers
metaData <- metadata %>%
  mutate(Layer = case_when(
    Depth %in% c("0-5 cm", "5-10 cm", "20-30 cm") ~ "Top-soil (0-30 cm)",
    Depth %in% c("40-50 cm", "80-100 cm") ~ "Bottom-soil (40-100 cm)",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # For unexpected values
  ))

rownames(metaData) <- metaData$Sample_ID
countData <- countData[, rownames(metaData)]

# Control if all Sample Ids are assigned correctly
all(colnames(countData) == rownames(metaData))

# DESeq2
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = countData, 
                              colData = metaData, 
                              design = ~Treatment)
dds

# Set reference level to ambient
dds$Treatment <- relevel(dds$Treatment, ref = "ambient")
dds <- DESeq(dds)
res <- results(dds)
head(results(dds, tidy=TRUE)) 
summary(res) 
res <- res[order(res$padj),]
head(res)

vsd <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds, blind = TRUE)
head(assay(vsd), 3)
```

## Step 2c: Subset DESeq 2 ggplot2 without NAS
```{r}
create_volcano_plot <- function(res, title) {
  df <- as.data.frame(res)
   df <- df[complete.cases(df), ]
  df$significant <- ifelse(df$padj < 0.05, 
                           ifelse(df$log2FoldChange < 0, "Down", "Up"), 
                           "Not Significant")
  
  ggplot(df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(pvalue), color = significant)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Down" = "blue", "Up" = "red", "Not Significant" = "gray")) +
    xlim(-3, 3) +
    ylim(0, 6) +
    labs(title = title,
         x = "log2 Fold Change",
         y = "-log10 p-value") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}

analyze_and_plot <- function(dds_subset, title_suffix) {
  dds_subset$Treatment <- relevel(dds_subset$Treatment, ref = "ambient")
  
  res_1.5 <- results(dds_subset, contrast = c("Treatment", "1.5", "ambient"))
  res_1.5 <- res_1.5[order(res_1.5$padj), ]
  
  res_3.0 <- results(dds_subset, contrast = c("Treatment", "3", "ambient"))
  res_3.0 <- res_3.0[order(res_3.0$padj), ]
  
  plot_1.5 <- create_volcano_plot(res_1.5, paste("+1.5°C vs Ambient -", title_suffix))
  plot_3.0 <- create_volcano_plot(res_3.0, paste("+3.0°C vs Ambient -", title_suffix))
  
  grid.arrange(plot_1.5, plot_3.0, ncol = 2)
}

plots_PZ <- analyze_and_plot(dds_PZ, "PZ")
plots_LM <- analyze_and_plot(dds_LM, "LM")
plots_HM <- analyze_and_plot(dds_HM, "HM")

grid.arrange(plots_PZ, plots_LM, plots_HM, ncol = 1)
```
## Step 2g: Extract significantly up/downregulated Genes
```{r}
analyze_and_plot <- function(dds_subset, zone) {
  dds_subset$Treatment <- relevel(dds_subset$Treatment, ref = "ambient")
  
  res_1.5 <- results(dds_subset, contrast = c("Treatment", "1.5", "ambient"))
  res_1.5 <- res_1.5[order(res_1.5$padj), ]
  
  res_3.0 <- results(dds_subset, contrast = c("Treatment", "3", "ambient"))
  res_3.0 <- res_3.0[order(res_3.0$padj), ]
  
  # Filter and add Zone and Treatment information
  res_table_1.5 <- res_1.5 %>% 
    data.frame() %>% 
    filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
    mutate(Zone = zone, Treatment = "1.5")
  
  res_table_3.0 <- res_3.0 %>% 
    data.frame() %>% 
    filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
    mutate(Zone = zone, Treatment = "3.0")
  
  # Combine the filtered results
  combined_results <- rbind(res_table_1.5, res_table_3.0)
  
  return(combined_results)
}

# Now you can run your analysis for each zone
results_PZ <- analyze_and_plot(dds_PZ, "PZ")
results_LM <- analyze_and_plot(dds_LM, "LM")
results_HM <- analyze_and_plot(dds_HM, "HM")

# Combine all results
final_results <- rbind(results_PZ, results_LM, results_HM)


final_results <- final_results %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "ASV") %>%
  select(ASV, everything())

# Merge final_results with taxonomy data

# First, convert rownames to a column in the asv dataframe
asv_with_ids <- asv %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "ASV")

# Now join with the final_results
final_results_with_taxonomy <- final_results %>%
  left_join(asv_with_ids, by = "ASV")

# Reorder columns if needed
final_results_with_taxonomy <- final_results_with_taxonomy %>%
  select(ASV, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, log2FoldChange, padj, Zone, Treatment)

# Save it to a CSV file
write.csv(final_results_with_taxonomy, "df_deseq2_genes.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```


```{r}

# Read the CSV file, which was previously generated and edited in Excel (add citations, functions)
df_genes_citation <- read.csv("df_deseq2_genes_citations.csv", sep = ";", dec = ".")

# Round padj and log2FoldChange to two decimal places and remove some columns
df_simplified <- df_genes_citation %>%
  mutate(
    padj = round(padj, 3),
    log2FoldChange = round(log2FoldChange, 3)
  )# %>%
  #select(-c(Kingdom, Source, pH, Additional)) # Remove these columns

# Create a simple table for up-regulated genes
up_regulated <- df_simplified %>% 
  filter(log2FoldChange > 0) %>%
  arrange(Zone, Treatment, desc(log2FoldChange))

# Display the table using gt
up_regulated %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Up-regulated Genes") %>%
  cols_align(align = "left") %>%
  tab_options(table.font.size = 12)

# Create a simple table for down-regulated genes
down_regulated <- df_simplified %>% 
  filter(log2FoldChange <= 0) %>%
  arrange(Zone, Treatment, desc(log2FoldChange))

# Display the table using gt
down_regulated %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Down-regulated Genes") %>%
  cols_align(align = "left") %>%
  tab_options(table.font.size = 12)

```


## Step 2d: DESeq2 on Layer Zone Treatment
```{r}
# Function to subset by Zone and Layer
subset_and_analyze <- function(dds, zone, layer) {
  dds_subset <- dds[, dds$Zone == zone & dds$Layer == layer]
  dds_subset <- DESeq(dds_subset)
  return(dds_subset)
}

# Subset and analyze for each Zone and Layer combination
dds_PZ_top <- subset_and_analyze(dds, "PZ", "Top-soil (0-30 cm)")
dds_PZ_bottom <- subset_and_analyze(dds, "PZ", "Bottom-soil (40-100 cm)")
dds_LM_top <- subset_and_analyze(dds, "LM", "Top-soil (0-30 cm)")
dds_LM_bottom <- subset_and_analyze(dds, "LM", "Bottom-soil (40-100 cm)")
dds_HM_top <- subset_and_analyze(dds, "HM", "Top-soil (0-30 cm)")
dds_HM_bottom <- subset_and_analyze(dds, "HM", "Bottom-soil (40-100 cm)")

# Function for volcano plots
create_volcano_plot <- function(res, title) {
  df <- as.data.frame(res)
  
  df <- df[complete.cases(df), ]
    
  df$significant <- ifelse(df$padj < 0.05, 
                           ifelse(df$log2FoldChange < 0, "Down", "Up"), 
                           "NS")
  
  ggplot(df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(pvalue), color = significant)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Down" = "blue", "Up" = "red", "NS" = "gray")) +
    xlim(-3, 3) +
    ylim(0, max(-log10(df$pvalue))) +
    labs(title = title, x = "log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10 p-value") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}

# Function for plotting
analyze_and_plot <- function(dds_subset, title_suffix) {
  dds_subset$Treatment <- relevel(dds_subset$Treatment, ref = "ambient")
  
  res_1.5 <- results(dds_subset, contrast = c("Treatment", "1.5", "ambient"))
  res_3.0 <- results(dds_subset, contrast = c("Treatment", "3", "ambient"))
  
  plot_1.5 <- create_volcano_plot(res_1.5, paste("+1.5°C vs Ambient -", title_suffix))
  plot_3.0 <- create_volcano_plot(res_3.0, paste("+3.0°C vs Ambient -", title_suffix))
  
  list(plot_1.5, plot_3.0)
}

# All plots
plots_PZ_top <- analyze_and_plot(dds_PZ_top, "PZ - Top-soil")
plots_PZ_bottom <- analyze_and_plot(dds_PZ_bottom, "PZ - Bottom-soil")
plots_LM_top <- analyze_and_plot(dds_LM_top, "LM - Top-soil")
plots_LM_bottom <- analyze_and_plot(dds_LM_bottom, "LM - Bottom-soil")
plots_HM_top <- analyze_and_plot(dds_HM_top, "HM - Top-soil")
plots_HM_bottom <- analyze_and_plot(dds_HM_bottom, "HM - Bottom-soil")

# Arranged Plot
all_plots <- c(plots_PZ_top, plots_PZ_bottom, 
               plots_LM_top, plots_LM_bottom, 
               plots_HM_top, plots_HM_bottom)

grid.arrange(grobs = all_plots, ncol = 2)
```

## Step 2e: DESeq 2 Heatmaps
```{r}
# Function to create heatmap
create_heatmap <- function(vsd, dds, title) {
  # Get top 10 ASVs
  assay_data <- assay(vsd)
  row_vars <- apply(assay_data, 1, var)
  top_10_asvs <- names(sort(row_vars, decreasing = TRUE)[1:10])
  top_10_data <- assay_data[top_10_asvs, ]
  
  # Prepare data for plotting
  data_long <- top_10_data %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rownames_to_column("ASV") %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -ASV, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Abundance")
  
  data_long$Treatment <- factor(colData(dds)$Treatment[match(data_long$Sample, colnames(assay_data))])
  
  # Create heatmap
  ggplot(data_long, aes(x = Sample, y = ASV, fill = Abundance)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_fill_viridis_c() +
    facet_grid(~ Treatment, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5),
          axis.title.x = element_blank(),
          panel.grid = element_blank()) +
    labs(title = title, y = "ASV", fill = "Abundance")
}

# All Plots
plot_PZ <- create_heatmap(vsd_PZ, dds_PZ, "Top 10 ASVs - Pioneer Zone")
plot_LM <- create_heatmap(vsd_LM, dds_LM, "Top 10 ASVs - Low Marsh")
plot_HM <- create_heatmap(vsd_HM, dds_HM, "Top 10 ASVs - High Marsh")

# Arranged plot
grid.arrange(plot_PZ, plot_LM, plot_HM, ncol = 1)
```



# Tax4Fun2 - Visualize Functional Predictions using ggpicrust2
Following: https://github.com/cafferychen777/ggpicrust2 
```{r}
install.packages("ggpicrust2")

if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")

pkgs <- c("phyloseq", "ALDEx2", "SummarizedExperiment", "Biobase", "devtools", 
          "ComplexHeatmap", "BiocGenerics", "BiocManager", "metagenomeSeq", 
          "Maaslin2", "edgeR", "lefser", "limma", "KEGGREST", "DESeq2")

for (pkg in pkgs) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE))
    BiocManager::install(pkg)
}

# Fix  fior ALDEx2
remove.packages("ALDEx2")
install.packages("archive.bioconductor.org_packages_3.15_bioc_src_contrib_Archive_ALDEx2_ALDEx2_1.28.0.tar.gz", repos = NULL, type="source")
library(ALDEx2)

# If you want to analyze the abundance of KEGG pathways instead of KO within the pathway, please set `ko_to_kegg` to TRUE.
# KEGG pathways typically have more descriptive explanations.

library(readr)
library(ggpicrust2)
library(tibble)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggprism)
library(patchwork)
# Load necessary data: abundance data and metadata

metadata <- read_delim(
    "K://PhD/data/WP2_microbial/GFZ_RNA_DNA/MERIT_RNA_repo/metadata.txt",
    delim = "\t",
    escape_double = FALSE,
    trim_ws = TRUE
)

# Load KEGG pathway abundance
# data(kegg_abundance)
file_path_all <- "K:/PhD/data/WP2_microbial/GFZ_RNA_DNA/MERIT_RNA_repo/pathway_prediction_raw.txt"
file_path_PZ <- "K:/PhD/data/WP2_microbial/GFZ_RNA_DNA/MERIT_RNA_repo/pathway_prediction_PZ.txt"
file_path_LM <- "K:/PhD/data/WP2_microbial/GFZ_RNA_DNA/MERIT_RNA_repo/pathway_prediction_LM.txt"
file_path_HM <- "K:/PhD/data/WP2_microbial/GFZ_RNA_DNA/MERIT_RNA_repo/pathway_prediction_HM.txt"

kegg_abundance <- read_delim(file_path_all, delim = "\t")
kegg_abundance_PZ <- read_delim(file_path_PZ, delim = "\t")
kegg_abundance_LM <- read_delim(file_path_LM, delim = "\t")
kegg_abundance_HM <- read_delim(file_path_HM, delim = "\t")

## PCA Plots pathway analysis
plot_pca_kegg<-pathway_pca(abundance = kegg_abundance , metadata = metadata, group = "Treatment") 
plot_pca_kegg_PZ<-pathway_pca(abundance = kegg_abundance_PZ, metadata = metadata, group = "Treatment") 
plot_pca_kegg_LM<-pathway_pca(abundance = kegg_abundance_LM , metadata = metadata, group = "Treatment") 
plot_pca_kegg_HM<-pathway_pca(abundance = kegg_abundance_HM, metadata = metadata, group = "Treatment") 

plot_pca_kegg
plot_pca_kegg_PZ
plot_pca_kegg_LM
plot_pca_kegg_HM
```
## Step 2b: DESeq 2 referenced to ambient T
```{r}
# Ensure the reference level is set correctly
dds$Treatment <- relevel(dds$Treatment, ref = "ambient")

# Run DESeq analysis
dds <- DESeq(dds)

# Extract results for +1.5°C vs ambient
res_1.5 <- results(dds, contrast = c("Treatment", "1.5", "ambient"))
res_1.5 <- res_1.5[order(res_1.5$padj), ]  

# Extract results for +3.0°C vs ambient
res_3.0 <- results(dds, contrast = c("Treatment", "3", "ambient"))
res_3.0 <- res_3.0[order(res_3.0$padj), ]  

# Plot for +1.5°C vs ambient
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))  

with(res_1.5, plot(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), pch = 20, 
                   main = "+1.5°C vs Ambient", xlim = c(-3, 3), col = "gray"))
with(subset(res_1.5, padj < 0.01), points(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), 
                                          pch = 20, col = "blue"))
with(subset(res_1.5, padj < 0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) > 1), points(log2FoldChange, 
                                                                   -log10(pvalue), pch = 20, col = "red"))

# Plot for +3.0°C vs ambient
with(res_3.0, plot(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), pch = 20, 
                   main = "+3.0°C vs Ambient", xlim = c(-3, 3), col = "gray"))
with(subset(res_3.0, padj < 0.01), points(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), 
                                          pch = 20, col = "blue"))
with(subset(res_3.0, padj < 0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) > 1), points(log2FoldChange, 
                                                                   -log10(pvalue), pch = 20, col = "red"))

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```



## Step 2b: DESeq 2 referenced to ambient T ggplot2
```{r}
# Function to create a volcano plot
create_volcano_plot <- function(res, title) {
  df <- as.data.frame(res)
  df$significant <- ifelse(df$padj < 0.05 & abs(df$log2FoldChange) > 1, "High", 
                           ifelse(df$padj < 0.01, "Medium", "Low"))
  
  ggplot(df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(pvalue), color = significant)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("High" = "red", "Medium" = "blue", "Low" = "gray")) +
    xlim(-3, 3) +
    ylim(0, 11) +
    labs(title = title,
         x = "log2 Fold Change",
         y = "-log10 p-value") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}

# Create plots
plot_1.5 <- create_volcano_plot(res_1.5, "+1.5°C vs Ambient")
plot_3.0 <- create_volcano_plot(res_3.0, "+3.0°C vs Ambient")

# Arrange plots side by side
grid.arrange(plot_1.5, plot_3.0, ncol = 2)
```

```{r}
# Check for missing information on different levels (Is there missing Genus or Phylum). Do we have negative counts
any(is.na(master_df$Genus))
any(is.na(master_df$Phylum))
any(is.na(master_df$count) | master_df$count < 0)

# Transfrom count into numeric format
master_df$count <- as.numeric(master_df$count)

# Create a new dataframe which is sorted by asv
df_asv <- reshape(data=master_df,  # Name des Datensatzes
                       v.names="count",
                       timevar= "asv",
                       idvar="Sample_ID", 
                       direction="wide")
# Exclude both MOCK communities (X138, X139) as well as the negative-DNA (X136) and negative-PCR (X137) controls
df_asv <- subset(df_asv, Sample_ID != "X136" & Sample_ID != "X137" & Sample_ID != "X138" & Sample_ID != "X139")

# Transform all coloumns with factors to factors and all Genus coloumns to numeric
df_asv <- df_asv %>%
  mutate_at(c(1:18), as.factor)

# Create a new dataframe which is sorted by Genus
df_gen <- reshape(data=master_df,  # Name des Datensatzes
                       v.names="count",
                       timevar= "Genus",
                       idvar="Sample_ID", 
                       direction="wide")
# Exclude both MOCK communities (X138, X139) as well as the negative-DNA (X136) and negative-PCR (X137) controls
df_gen <- subset(df_gen, Sample_ID != "X136" & Sample_ID != "X137" & Sample_ID != "X138" & Sample_ID != "X139")

# Transform all coloumns with factors to factors and all Genus coloumns to numeric
df_genus <- df_gen %>%
  mutate_at(c(1:18), as.factor)
```

## Step 2b: Subset DESeq 2
```{r}
# Subset by Zone: PZ
dds_PZ <- dds[, dds$Zone == "PZ"]
dds_PZ <- DESeq(dds_PZ)
vsd_PZ <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds_PZ, blind = TRUE)

# Subset by Zone: LM
dds_LM <- dds[, dds$Zone == "LM"]
dds_LM <- DESeq(dds_LM)
vsd_LM <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds_LM, blind = TRUE)

# Subset by Zone: HM
dds_HM <- dds[, dds$Zone == "HM"]
dds_HM <- DESeq(dds_HM)
vsd_HM <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds_HM, blind = TRUE)

# Function for Analysis and Plotting
analyze_and_plot <- function(dds_subset, title_suffix) {
  dds_subset$Treatment <- relevel(dds_subset$Treatment, ref = "ambient")
  
  # Extract results for +1.5°C vs ambient
  res_1.5 <- results(dds_subset, contrast = c("Treatment", "1.5", "ambient"))
  res_1.5 <- res_1.5[order(res_1.5$padj), ]
  
  # Extract results for +3.0°C vs ambient
  res_3.0 <- results(dds_subset, contrast = c("Treatment", "3", "ambient"))
  res_3.0 <- res_3.0[order(res_3.0$padj), ]
  
  # Plot results
  par(mfrow = c(1, 2))  # Arrange plots side by side
  
  # +1.5°C vs ambient
  with(res_1.5, plot(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), pch = 20, 
                     main = paste("+1.5°C vs Ambient -", title_suffix), 
                     xlim = c(-3, 3), col = "gray"))
  with(subset(res_1.5, padj < 0.01), points(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), 
                                            pch = 20, col = "blue"))
  with(subset(res_1.5, padj < 0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) > 1), points(log2FoldChange, 
                                                                     -log10(pvalue), pch = 20, col = "red"))
  # +3.0°C vs ambient
  with(res_3.0, plot(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), pch = 20, 
                     main = paste("+3.0°C vs Ambient -", title_suffix), 
                     xlim = c(-3, 3), col = "gray"))
  with(subset(res_3.0, padj < 0.01), points(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), 
                                            pch = 20, col = "blue"))
  with(subset(res_3.0, padj < 0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) > 1), points(log2FoldChange, 
                                                                     -log10(pvalue), pch = 20, col = "red"))
  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
}

# Run analysis and plotting for each subset
analyze_and_plot(dds_PZ, "PZ")
analyze_and_plot(dds_LM, "LM")
analyze_and_plot(dds_HM, "HM")
```

## Step 2e: Subset DESeq 2 ggplot2
```{r}
create_volcano_plot <- function(res, title) {
  df <- as.data.frame(res)
  df$significant <- ifelse(df$padj < 0.05, 
                           ifelse(df$log2FoldChange < 0, "Down", "Up"), 
                           "Not Significant")
  
  ggplot(df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(pvalue), color = significant)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Down" = "blue", "Up" = "red", "Not Significant" = "gray")) +
    xlim(-3, 3) +
    ylim(0, 6) +
    labs(title = title,
         x = "log2 Fold Change",
         y = "-log10 p-value") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}

analyze_and_plot <- function(dds_subset, title_suffix) {
  dds_subset$Treatment <- relevel(dds_subset$Treatment, ref = "ambient")
  
  res_1.5 <- results(dds_subset, contrast = c("Treatment", "1.5", "ambient"))
  res_1.5 <- res_1.5[order(res_1.5$padj), ]
  
  res_3.0 <- results(dds_subset, contrast = c("Treatment", "3", "ambient"))
  res_3.0 <- res_3.0[order(res_3.0$padj), ]
  
  plot_1.5 <- create_volcano_plot(res_1.5, paste("+1.5°C vs Ambient -", title_suffix))
  plot_3.0 <- create_volcano_plot(res_3.0, paste("+3.0°C vs Ambient -", title_suffix))
  
  grid.arrange(plot_1.5, plot_3.0, ncol = 2)
}

plots_PZ <- analyze_and_plot(dds_PZ, "PZ")
plots_LM <- analyze_and_plot(dds_LM, "LM")
plots_HM <- analyze_and_plot(dds_HM, "HM")

grid.arrange(plots_PZ, plots_LM, plots_HM, ncol = 1)
```




```{r}
# Function to get the most detailed taxonomic information
get_detailed_taxonomy <- function(asv_id, asv_data) {
  tax_ranks <- c("Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus")
  tax_info <- asv_data[asv_id, tax_ranks]
  
  for (rank in rev(tax_ranks)) {
    if (!is.na(tax_info[rank]) && tax_info[rank] != "") {
      return(paste(rank, tax_info[rank], sep=": "))
    }
  }
  return(asv_id)  # Return ASV ID if no taxonomy info available
}

# Function to get significant ASVs and prepare data
prepare_sig_asvs_data <- function(vsd, dds, asv_data, alpha = 0.05) {
  # Get results for all comparisons
  res_1.5 <- results(dds, contrast = c("Treatment", "1.5", "ambient"))
  res_3.0 <- results(dds, contrast = c("Treatment", "3", "ambient"))
  
  # Combine significant ASVs from both comparisons
  sig_asvs <- unique(c(
    rownames(res_1.5)[which(res_1.5$padj < alpha)],
    rownames(res_3.0)[which(res_3.0$padj < alpha)]
  ))
  
  if(length(sig_asvs) == 0) {
    return(NULL)  # Return NULL if no significant ASVs
  }
  
  # Subset data for significant ASVs
  sig_data <- assay(vsd)[sig_asvs, ]
  
  row_names <- sapply(sig_asvs, get_detailed_taxonomy, asv_data)
  rownames(sig_data) <- row_names
  
  df_annot <- as.data.frame(colData(dds)[, c("Zone", "Treatment")])
  df_annot$Treatment <- factor(df_annot$Treatment, levels = c("ambient", "1.5", "3"))
  
  sample_order <- order(df_annot$Treatment)
  sig_data_ordered <- sig_data[, sample_order]
  df_annot_ordered <- df_annot[sample_order, ]
  
  return(list(data = sig_data_ordered, annot = df_annot_ordered))
}

# Prepare data for all zones
data_HM <- prepare_sig_asvs_data(vsd_HM, dds_HM, asv)
data_LM <- prepare_sig_asvs_data(vsd_LM, dds_LM, asv)
data_PZ <- prepare_sig_asvs_data(vsd_PZ, dds_PZ, asv)

# Calculate common color scale for non-null datasets
all_values <- c()
for(data in list(data_HM, data_LM, data_PZ)) {
  if(!is.null(data)) {
    all_values <- c(all_values, data$data)
  }
}
color_breaks <- seq(min(all_values), max(all_values), length.out = 100)

# Define custom colors for Treatment
treatment_colors <- c("ambient" = "#4575B4", "1.5" = "#FDAE61", "3" = "#D73027")

# Function to plot heatmap with common color scale and custom annotation colors
plot_heatmap <- function(data, annot, zone_name, color_breaks, treatment_colors) {
  if(is.null(data)) {
    cat("No significant ASVs found for", zone_name, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  pheatmap(data,
           cluster_rows = TRUE,
           show_rownames = TRUE,
           cluster_cols = FALSE,
           annotation_col = annot,
           annotation_colors = list(Treatment = treatment_colors),
           main = paste("Significant ASVs -", zone_name),
           breaks = color_breaks)
}

# Plot heatmaps for each zone with common color scale and custom annotation colors
plot_heatmap(data_HM$data, data_HM$annot, "High Marsh", color_breaks, treatment_colors)
plot_heatmap(data_LM$data, data_LM$annot, "Low Marsh", color_breaks, treatment_colors)
plot_heatmap(data_PZ$data, data_PZ$annot, "Pioneer Zone", color_breaks, treatment_colors)



# Function to check and correct sample assignments
correct_sample_assignments <- function(dds) {
  # Get the sample names from the count data
  count_samples <- colnames(counts(dds))
  
  # Get the sample names from colData
  coldata_samples <- rownames(colData(dds))
  
  # Check if they match
  if (!all(count_samples == coldata_samples)) {
    warning("Sample names in counts and colData do not match. Attempting to correct...")
    
    # Reorder colData to match count data
    colData(dds) <- colData(dds)[count_samples,]
  }
  
  return(dds)
}

# Apply the correction to each DDS object
dds_HM <- correct_sample_assignments(dds_HM)
dds_LM <- correct_sample_assignments(dds_LM)
dds_PZ <- correct_sample_assignments(dds_PZ)

```

## Step 2e: DESeq 2 Gene changes
```{r}
# Function to extract and plot top genes for each Zone and Treatment comparison
analyze_top_genes <- function(dds_subset, zone_name, contrast, n_top = 6) {
  # Extract results for the given contrast
  res <- results(dds_subset, contrast = contrast)
  res <- res[order(res$padj), ]  # Sort by adjusted p-value
  
  # Select the most relevant genes (highest absolute log2FoldChange)
  res <- as.data.frame(res)
  res$ASV <- rownames(res)  # Add ASV names
  top_genes <- res %>%
    filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%  # Remove rows with NA padj
    arrange(desc(abs(log2FoldChange))) %>%  # Sort by absolute log2FoldChange
    head(n_top)  # Take the top n genes
  
  # Display top genes
  print(paste("Top genes for", zone_name, "(", paste(contrast, collapse = " vs "), "):"))
  print(top_genes[, c("ASV", "log2FoldChange", "padj")])
  
  # Plot counts for top genes
  par(mfrow = c(2, 3))  # Adjust plotting layout
  for (gene in top_genes$ASV) {
    plotCounts(dds_subset, gene = gene, intgroup = "Treatment",
               main = paste("Gene:", gene))
  }
  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))  # Reset plotting layout
}

# Subset and analyze each Zone
for (zone in levels(metaData$Zone)) {
  dds_zone <- dds[, dds$Zone == zone]  # Subset the DESeqDataSet for the Zone
  dds_zone <- DESeq(dds_zone)  # Run DESeq for the subset
  
  # Analyze for +1.5°C vs ambient
  analyze_top_genes(dds_zone, zone_name = zone, contrast = c("Treatment", "1.5", "ambient"))
  
  # Analyze for +3.0°C vs ambient
  analyze_top_genes(dds_zone, zone_name = zone, contrast = c("Treatment", "3", "ambient"))
}

```

```{r}
# High Marsh
assay_data_HM <- assay(vsd_HM)
row_vars_HM <- apply(assay_data_HM, 1, var)
top_10_asvs_HM <- names(sort(row_vars_HM, decreasing = TRUE)[1:10])
top_10_data_HM <- assay_data_HM[top_10_asvs_HM, ]

data_HM <- top_10_data_HM %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("ASV") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -ASV, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Abundance")

data_HM$Treatment <- factor(colData(dds_HM)$Treatment[match(data_HM$Sample, colnames(assay_data_HM))])

# Plot High Marsh
plot_HM <- ggplot(data_HM, aes(x = Sample, y = ASV, fill = Abundance)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  facet_grid(~ Treatment, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank()) +
  labs(title = "Top 10 ASVs - High Marsh", y = "ASV", fill = "Abundance")

print(plot_HM)

# Low Marsh
assay_data_LM <- assay(vsd_LM)
row_vars_LM <- apply(assay_data_LM, 1, var)
top_10_asvs_LM <- names(sort(row_vars_LM, decreasing = TRUE)[1:10])
top_10_data_LM <- assay_data_LM[top_10_asvs_LM, ]

data_LM <- top_10_data_LM %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("ASV") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -ASV, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Abundance")

data_LM$Treatment <- factor(colData(dds_LM)$Treatment[match(data_LM$Sample, colnames(assay_data_LM))])

# Plot Low Marsh
plot_LM <- ggplot(data_LM, aes(x = Sample, y = ASV, fill = Abundance)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  facet_grid(~ Treatment, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank()) +
  labs(title = "Top 10 ASVs - Low Marsh", y = "ASV", fill = "Abundance")

print(plot_LM)

# Pioneer Zone
assay_data_PZ <- assay(vsd_PZ)
row_vars_PZ <- apply(assay_data_PZ, 1, var)
top_10_asvs_PZ <- names(sort(row_vars_PZ, decreasing = TRUE)[1:10])
top_10_data_PZ <- assay_data_PZ[top_10_asvs_PZ, ]

data_PZ <- top_10_data_PZ %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("ASV") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -ASV, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Abundance")

data_PZ$Treatment <- factor(colData(dds_PZ)$Treatment[match(data_PZ$Sample, colnames(assay_data_PZ))])

# Plot Pioneer Zone
plot_PZ <- ggplot(data_PZ, aes(x = Sample, y = ASV, fill = Abundance)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  facet_grid(~ Treatment, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank()) +
  labs(title = "Top 10 ASVs - Pioneer Zone", y = "ASV", fill = "Abundance")

print(plot_PZ)
```

# Step 7: Bubble Plots
## Step 7a: Family table transformation
```{r}
# Remove specified samples
samples_to_remove <- c("X136", "X137", "X138", "X139")
tab_fam_filtered <- tab_fam %>%
  select(-all_of(samples_to_remove))

## Transform and filter data
tab_fam_long <- tab_fam_filtered %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -1, names_to = "Sample_ID", values_to = "Count") %>%
  rename(full_taxonomy = 1) %>%
  filter(!str_ends(full_taxonomy, "others;")) %>%
  filter(!str_ends(full_taxonomy, "others")) %>%
  mutate(last_taxa = str_extract(full_taxonomy, "[^;]+$"))

# Merge with metadata and calculate relative abundance
tab_fam_agg <- tab_fam_long %>%
  left_join(metadata, by = "Sample_ID") %>%
  group_by(Sample_ID) %>%
  mutate(TotalCount = sum(Count)) %>%
  group_by(last_taxa, Treatment, Zone) %>%
  summarise(RelativeAbundance = sum(Count) / sum(TotalCount), .groups = "drop")

# Select top N most abundant families
N <- 25  # Change this to the number of top families you want to show
top_families <- tab_fam_agg %>%
  group_by(last_taxa) %>%
  summarise(TotalRelativeAbundance = sum(RelativeAbundance)) %>%
  top_n(N, TotalRelativeAbundance) %>%
  pull(last_taxa)

# Filter for top families
tab_fam_agg_filtered <- tab_fam_agg %>%
  filter(last_taxa %in% top_families)

# Set factor levels for Zone and Treatment
tab_fam_agg_filtered$Zone <- factor(tab_fam_agg_filtered$Zone, levels = c("PZ", "LM", "HM"))
tab_fam_agg_filtered$Treatment <- recode_factor(tab_fam_agg_filtered$Treatment, "ambient" = "ambient", "1.5" = "+1.5°C", "3"  = "+3.0°C")

# Create the bubble plot
plot_bubble_fam <- ggplot(tab_fam_agg_filtered, aes(x = Treatment, y = last_taxa, size = RelativeAbundance, color = Treatment)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_size(range = c(1, 10), name = "Relative Abundance") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("ambient" = "#4575B4", "+1.5°C" = "#FDAE61", "+3.0°C" = "#D73027")) +
  facet_grid(. ~ Zone, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  theme_JM +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title = element_blank(),
        strip.placement = "outside") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = "")

# Summary table of most abundant families
top_families_summary <- tab_fam_agg_filtered %>%
  group_by(last_taxa) %>%
  summarise(MeanRelativeAbundance = mean(RelativeAbundance)) %>%
  arrange(factor(last_taxa)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 25)

# Write the summary table to a CSV file
write.csv(top_families_summary, "df_top_25_families.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# All plots
plot_bubble_fam
```

# NMDS Plot
```{r,  message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Set color parameters
polygon_colors      <-  c("blue","#FDAE61","#D73027") 
my_palette <- c("#4575B4", "#FDAE61", "#D73027")
my_palette2 <- c("tan", "darkgreen", "lightgreen")
# Build matrix used for NMDS Plots (Plot https://jkzorz.github.io/2019/06/06/NMDS.html)
com = df_asv[,20:ncol(df_asv)]
m_com = as.matrix(com)

# Transform matrix values to be numeric and remove any NAs
m_com <- apply(m_com, 2, as.numeric)
any(is.na(m_com))
m_com <- m_com[complete.cases(m_com), ]
any(is.na(m_com))
m_com <- na.omit(m_com)

set.seed(123)
nmds = metaMDS(m_com, distance = "bray", na.rm = TRUE)
nmds  # Stress is 0.186 
plot(nmds)

# Build matrix used for NMDS Plot on Zone specific subsets
df_asv_PZ <- df_asv%>%
  filter(Zone =="PZ")

com_PZ = df_asv_PZ[,20:ncol(df_asv_PZ)]
m_com_PZ = as.matrix(com_PZ)

set.seed(123)
nmds_PZ = metaMDS(m_com_PZ, distance = "bray", na.rm = TRUE)
nmds_PZ  # Stress is 0.1536 
plot(nmds_PZ)

# Adjust factors for correct/desired order in plots
nmds_data           <- data.frame(nmds$points, Depth = df_asv$Depth, Treatment = df_asv$Treatment, Zone = df_asv$Zone)
nmds_data_PZ           <- data.frame(nmds_PZ$points, Depth = df_asv_PZ$Depth, Treatment = df_asv_PZ$Treatment)

nmds_data$Depth     <- factor(nmds_data$Depth, levels = c("0-5 cm", "5-10 cm", "20-30 cm", "40-50 cm", "80-100 cm"))
nmds_data$Treatment <- as.factor(nmds_data$Treatment)
nmds_data$Zone      <- factor(nmds_data$Zone, levels = c("PZ","LM","HM"))
nmds_data$Treatment <- recode_factor(nmds_data$Treatment,  "0"="ambient", "1.5"="+1.5°C","3"="+3.0°C")

nmds_data_PZ$Depth     <- factor(nmds_data_PZ$Depth, levels = c("0-5 cm", "5-10 cm", "20-30 cm", "40-50 cm", "80-100 cm"))
nmds_data_PZ$Treatment <- recode_factor(nmds_data_PZ$Treatment,  "0"="ambient", "1.5"="+1.5°C","3"="+3.0°C")

# NMDS Treatment*Zone
plot_MDS1<-ggplot(nmds_data, aes(x = MDS1, y = MDS2)) +
  geom_point(size=3, aes( shape = Zone, colour = Treatment))+ 
  stat_ellipse(type = "t", level = 0.95, geom = "polygon", aes(fill = Treatment), alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = polygon_colors,name = "Warming treatment:") +
  theme_JM+
  xlab("NMDS 1")+
  ylab("NMDS 2")+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#4575B4", "#FDAE61","#D73027"),name = "Warming treatment:")+
  facet_grid(~Zone)

# NMDS Treatment*Zone*Depth
plot_MDS2<-ggplot(nmds_data, aes(x = MDS1, y = MDS2)) +
  geom_point(size=3, aes( shape = Zone, colour = Treatment))+ 
  stat_ellipse(type = "t", level = 0.95, geom = "polygon", aes(fill = Treatment), alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = polygon_colors) +
  theme_JM+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#4575B4", "#FDAE61","#D73027"), name = "Warming treatment:")+
  facet_grid(~Depth)

# NMDS Treatment*Zone*Depth v. 2
plot_MDS3<-ggplot(nmds_data, aes(x = MDS1, y = MDS2)) +
  geom_point(size=3, aes( shape = Zone, colour = Treatment))+ 
  stat_ellipse(type = "t", level = 0.95, geom = "polygon", aes(fill = Treatment), alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = polygon_colors) +
  theme_JM+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#4575B4", "#FDAE61","#D73027"))+
  facet_wrap(~Zone*Depth, ncol=5)

# NMDS PZ subset 
plot_MDS_PZ<-ggplot(nmds_data_PZ, aes(x = MDS1, y = MDS2)) +
  geom_point(size=3, aes( shape = Treatment, colour = Treatment))+ 
  stat_ellipse(type = "t", level = 0.95, geom = "polygon", aes(fill = Treatment), alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = polygon_colors) +
  theme_JM+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#4575B4", "#FDAE61","#D73027"))+
  facet_grid(~Depth)

plot_MDS1
plot_MDS2
plot_MDS3
plot_MDS_PZ
```
\pagebreak
# Statistical testing - PERMANOVA
```{r,  message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
permanova <- adonis2(m_com ~ Zone*Treatment*Depth, data = df_asv, permutations = 999)
permanova

# Run pairwise PERMANOVA tests
pairwise_perm <- pairwise.adonis2(m_com ~ Treatment*Zone*Depth, data = df_asv, 
                                  distance = "bray", method = "bray",
                                  p.adjust.method = "BH", permutations = 999)

# Run pairwise PERMANOVA tests for each factor
pairwise_perm_Zone <- pairwise.adonis2(m_com ~ Zone, data = df_asv, 
                                       distance = "bray", method = "bray",
                                       p.adjust.method = "BH", permutations = 999)
pairwise_perm_Treatment <- pairwise.adonis2(m_com ~ Treatment, data = df_asv, 
                                            distance = "bray", method = "bray",
                                            p.adjust.method = "BH", permutations = 999)
pairwise_perm_Depth <- pairwise.adonis2(m_com ~ Depth, data = df_asv, 
                                        distance = "bray", method = "bray",
                                        p.adjust.method = "BH", permutations = 999)

pairwise_perm_Treatment_PZ <- pairwise.adonis2(m_com_PZ ~ Treatment*Depth, data = df_asv_PZ, 
                                            distance = "bray", method = "bray",
                                            p.adjust.method = "BH", permutations = 999)
#pairwise_perm_Treatment_LM <- pairwise.adonis2(m_com_LM ~ Treatment, data = df_asv_PZ, 
#                                            distance = "bray", method = "bray",
 #                                           p.adjust.method = "BH", permutations = 999)
#pairwise_perm_Treatment_HM <- pairwise.adonis2(m_com_HM ~ Treatment, data = df_asv_PZ, 
  #                                          distance = "bray", method = "bray",
   #                                         p.adjust.method = "BH", permutations = 999)

# Results
pairwise_perm
pairwise_perm_Zone
pairwise_perm_Treatment
pairwise_perm_Depth
pairwise_perm_Treatment_PZ
```

# Bubble Plots
```{r,  message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#Transform asv table into long-format. Transformation will catch all headers (sample names) into a new coloumn (Sample_ID). This is done for all dataframes (Phylum, Order, Genus)
taxa_sum_Phylum <- taxa_sum_Phylum %>%                      
  gather(key = "Sample_ID", value = "count",X1, X2, X3, X4, X5, X6, X7, X8, X9, X10, X11, X12, X13, X14, X15, X16, X17, X18, X19, X20, X21, X22, X23, X24, X25, X26, X27, X28, X29, X30, X31, X32, X33, X34, X35, X36, X37, X38, X39, X40, X41, X42, X43, X44, X45, X46, X47, X48, X49, X50, X51, X52, X53, X54, X55, X56, X57, X58, X59, X60, X61, X62, X63, X64, X65, X66, X67, X68, X69, X70, X71, X72, X73, X74, X75, X76, X77, X78, X79, X80, X81, X82, X83, X84, X85, X86, X87, X88, X89, X90, X91, X92, X93, X94, X95, X96, X97, X98, X99, X100, X101, X102, X103, X104, X105, X106, X107, X108, X109, X110, X111, X112, X113, X114, X115, X116, X117, X118, X119, X120, X121, X122, X123, X124, X125, X126, X127, X128, X129, X130, X131, X132, X133, X134, X135, X136, X137, X138, X139)

taxa_sum_Order <- taxa_sum_Order %>%                      
  gather(key = "Sample_ID", value = "count",X1, X2, X3, X4, X5, X6, X7, X8, X9, X10, X11, X12, X13, X14, X15, X16, X17, X18, X19, X20, X21, X22, X23, X24, X25, X26, X27, X28, X29, X30, X31, X32, X33, X34, X35, X36, X37, X38, X39, X40, X41, X42, X43, X44, X45, X46, X47, X48, X49, X50, X51, X52, X53, X54, X55, X56, X57, X58, X59, X60, X61, X62, X63, X64, X65, X66, X67, X68, X69, X70, X71, X72, X73, X74, X75, X76, X77, X78, X79, X80, X81, X82, X83, X84, X85, X86, X87, X88, X89, X90, X91, X92, X93, X94, X95, X96, X97, X98, X99, X100, X101, X102, X103, X104, X105, X106, X107, X108, X109, X110, X111, X112, X113, X114, X115, X116, X117, X118, X119, X120, X121, X122, X123, X124, X125, X126, X127, X128, X129, X130, X131, X132, X133, X134, X135, X136, X137, X138, X139)

taxa_sum_Genus <- taxa_sum_Genus %>%                      
  gather(key = "Sample_ID", value = "count",X1, X2, X3, X4, X5, X6, X7, X8, X9, X10, X11, X12, X13, X14, X15, X16, X17, X18, X19, X20, X21, X22, X23, X24, X25, X26, X27, X28, X29, X30, X31, X32, X33, X34, X35, X36, X37, X38, X39, X40, X41, X42, X43, X44, X45, X46, X47, X48, X49, X50, X51, X52, X53, X54, X55, X56, X57, X58, X59, X60, X61, X62, X63, X64, X65, X66, X67, X68, X69, X70, X71, X72, X73, X74, X75, X76, X77, X78, X79, X80, X81, X82, X83, X84, X85, X86, X87, X88, X89, X90, X91, X92, X93, X94, X95, X96, X97, X98, X99, X100, X101, X102, X103, X104, X105, X106, X107, X108, X109, X110, X111, X112, X113, X114, X115, X116, X117, X118, X119, X120, X121, X122, X123, X124, X125, X126, X127, X128, X129, X130, X131, X132, X133, X134, X135, X136, X137, X138, X139)

# Merge asv table with metadata file to create a master dataframe (master_df)
taxa_sum_Phylum <- merge(taxa_sum_Phylum, metadata, by = "Sample_ID", all = TRUE)
taxa_sum_Order  <- merge(taxa_sum_Order , metadata, by = "Sample_ID", all = TRUE)
taxa_sum_Genus  <- merge(taxa_sum_Genus , metadata, by = "Sample_ID", all = TRUE)

# Exclude both MOCK communities (X138, X139) as well as the negative-DNA (X136) and negative-PCR (X137) controls
taxa_sum_Phylum <- subset(taxa_sum_Phylum, Sample_ID != "X136" & Sample_ID != "X137" & Sample_ID != "X138" & Sample_ID != "X139")
taxa_sum_Order  <- subset(taxa_sum_Order , Sample_ID != "X136" & Sample_ID != "X137" & Sample_ID != "X138" & Sample_ID != "X139")
taxa_sum_Genus  <- subset(taxa_sum_Genus , Sample_ID != "X136" & Sample_ID != "X137" & Sample_ID != "X138" & Sample_ID != "X139")

# Transform all coloumns with factors to factors and all Genus coloumns to numeric
taxa_sum_Phylum <- taxa_sum_Phylum %>%
  mutate_at(c(4:14), as.factor)%>%
  mutate_at(c(3), as.numeric)

taxa_sum_Order <- taxa_sum_Order%>%
  mutate_at(c(4:14), as.factor)%>%
  mutate_at(c(3), as.numeric)

taxa_sum_Genus <- taxa_sum_Genus%>%
  mutate_at(c(4:14), as.factor)%>%
  mutate_at(c(3), as.numeric)

# Create individual coloumns for the rank information currently stored in one coloumn "taxa"
taxa_sum_Order <- separate(taxa_sum_Order, taxa, into = c("Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order"), sep = ";", remove = FALSE)
taxa_sum_Genus <- separate(taxa_sum_Genus, taxa, into = c("Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus"), sep = ";", remove = FALSE)

# Bubble plots for Phylum level
taxa_sum_Phylum$Treatment <- recode_factor(taxa_sum_Phylum$Treatment ,  "0"="ambient", "1.5"="+1.5°C","3"="+3.0°C")

taxa_sum_Phylum_all<- taxa_sum_Phylum %>%
  group_by(Zone, Treatment, taxa, Depth) %>%
  summarize(count = sum(count))

plot_Bubble_Phylum <-  ggplot(taxa_sum_Phylum_all, aes(x=Treatment, y=taxa, size=count)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.7, show.legend =FALSE) +
  scale_size(range=c(2, 10)) +
  labs(x = "Phylum-Zone", y = "Relative abundance", title = "Relative abundance of microbial species") +
  theme_pubclean()

plot_Bubble_Phylum + facet_grid(~Zone*Depth)

# Filter phyla with less than 1 count
filtered_taxa_sum_Phylum<- taxa_sum_Phylum %>%
  group_by(Zone, Treatment, taxa, Depth) %>%
  summarize(count = sum(count)) %>%
 filter(count >= 1) 
  

filtered_taxa_sum_Phylum$Depth     <- factor(filtered_taxa_sum_Phylum$Depth, levels = c("0-5 cm", "5-10 cm", "20-30 cm", "40-50 cm", "80-100 cm"))
filtered_taxa_sum_Phylum$Zone    <- factor(filtered_taxa_sum_Phylum$Zone, levels = c("PZ","LM","HM"))

plot_Bubble_Phylum2 <-  ggplot(filtered_taxa_sum_Phylum, aes(x=Treatment, y=taxa, size=count, color=taxa)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.7, show.legend =FALSE) +
  scale_size(range=c(2, 10)) +
  labs(x = "Phylum-Zone", y = "Relative abundance", title = "Relative abundance of microbial species") +
  theme_JM+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

plot_Bubble_Phylum2 + facet_grid(~Zone*Depth)

# Bubble plots for Order dataframe with specific phylum levels
sum_proteo <- taxa_sum_Order %>% filter(Phylum == "Proteobacteria")
sum_proteo <- sum_proteo %>%
  group_by(Zone, Treatment, Depth,Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order) %>%
  summarize(count =sum(count))

sum_proteo <- sum_proteo %>%
  mutate_at(c(3:6), as.factor)%>%
   filter(count >= 1)

sum_proteo$Depth     <- factor(sum_proteo$Depth, levels = c("0-5 cm", "5-10 cm", "20-30 cm", "40-50 cm", "80-100 cm"))
sum_proteo$Zone      <- factor(sum_proteo$Zone, levels = c("PZ","LM","HM"))
sum_proteo$Class     <- factor(sum_proteo$Class, levels = c("Alphaproteobacteria","Gammaproteobacteria","Magnetococcia","Zetaproteobacteria","others" ))
sum_proteo$Treatment <- recode_factor(sum_proteo$Treatment ,  "0"="ambient", "1.5"="+1.5°C","3"="+3.0°C")

plot_Bubble_Proteo <-  ggplot(sum_proteo, aes(x=Treatment, y=Order, size=count, color=Treatment)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.7, show.legend =FALSE) +
  scale_size(range=c(2, 10)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c( "#4575B4", "#FDAE61", "#D73027")) +
  labs(x = "Phylum-Zone", y = "Relative abundance", title = "Relative abundance of microbial species") +
  theme_JM+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

plot_Bubble_Proteo+ facet_wrap(~Zone*Depth, ncol=5)

# Bubble plots for Genus level
# Filter dataset to only include methane relevant Genus, based on Yang, S., Anthony, S. E., Jenrich, M., in ’t Zandt, M. H., Strauss, J., Overduin, P. P., Grosse, G., Angelopoulos, M., Biskaborn, B. K., Grigoriev, M. N., Wagner, D., Knoblauch, C., Jaeschke, A., Rethemeyer, J., Kallmeyer, J., & Liebner, S. (2023). Microbial methane cycling in sediments of Arctic thermokarst lagoons. Global Change Biology, 29(10), 2714–2731. https://doi.org/10.1111/gcb.16649

# All hydrogenotrophic methanogens
Hyd_MOs <- list(c("Methanobacterium","Methanobrevibacter","Methanosphaera","Methanocellales","Methanomicrobiaceae","Methanoregula","Rice-Cluster-II"))

# All acetoclastic methanogens
Ace_MOs <- list(c("Methanosaeta","Methanosarcina"))

# All methylotrophic methanogens
Met_MOs <- list(c("Ca. Methanomethylicus","Methanofastidiosales","Methanomassiliicoccales","Methanoassiliicoccaceae"))

# All Methane oxidating bacteria (MOB Type 1)
MOB_MOs <- list(c("Methylomonadaceae","Crenothrix","Methylobacter","Methyloglobulus","Methyloprofundus","Methylosarcina","Methylovulum","Methylophagaceae","Methyloacidiphilaceae"))

# All anaerobic methane oxidizing bacteria (Canonical ANME & Noncanonical putative AOM)
AMOB_MOs <- list(c("ANME-2a-2b","Ca. Methanoperedens","ANME-3","Methylomirabilaceae","Ca. Methylomirabilis","Sh765B-TzT-35","Z114MB74"))

df_methane_MOs <- taxa_sum_Genus %>%
  filter(Genus %in% c(Hyd_MOs, Ace_MOs, Met_MOs, MOB_MOs, AMOB_MOs)) %>%
  mutate(pathway = case_when(
    Genus %in% Hyd_MOs ~ "Hydrogenotrophic methanogens",
    Genus %in% Ace_MOs ~ "Acetoclastic methanogens",
    Genus %in% Met_MOs ~ "Methylotrophic methanogens",
    Genus %in% MOB_MOs ~ "Methane oxidizing bacteria",
    Genus %in% AMOB_MOs ~ "Anaerobic methane oxidizing bacteria",
    TRUE ~ "other"  # Optional: Assign "other" for KO numbers not matching any pathway
  ))

df_methane_MOs_sum <- df_methane_MOs %>%
  group_by(Zone, Treatment, Depth,Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus) %>%
  summarize(count =sum(count))

df_methane_MOs_sum   <- df_methane_MOs_sum   %>%
  mutate_at(c(3:6), as.factor)%>%
   filter(count >= 1)

df_methane_MOs_sum$Depth     <- factor(df_methane_MOs_sum $Depth, levels = c("0-5 cm", "5-10 cm", "20-30 cm", "40-50 cm", "80-100 cm"))
df_methane_MOs_sum $Zone      <- factor(df_methane_MOs_sum $Zone, levels = c("PZ","LM","HM"))
df_methane_MOs_sum $Treatment <- recode_factor(df_methane_MOs_sum $Treatment ,  "0"="ambient", "1.5"="+1.5°C","3"="+3.0°C")

plot_methane_MOs <-  ggplot(df_methane_MOs_sum , aes(x=Treatment, y=Genus, size=count, color=Treatment)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.7, show.legend =FALSE) +
  scale_size(range=c(2, 10)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c( "#4575B4", "#FDAE61", "#D73027")) +
  labs(x = "Genus-Zone", y = "Relative abundance", title = "Relative abundance of microbial species") +
  theme_JM+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

plot_methane_MOs+ facet_wrap(~Zone*Depth, ncol=5)
```
\pagebreak
  
# Alpha Diversity 
```{r,message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Create a phyloseq object
a_tab = otu_table(as.matrix(tab[,1:(ncol(tab)-6)]), taxa_are_rows=T) 
t_tab = tax_table(as.matrix(tab[,(ncol(tab)-5):ncol(tab)])) 
metadata <- metadata %>%
  mutate_at(c(3:12), as.factor)
df_samp = sample_data(metadata) 
sample_ids <- metadata[, 1]
rownames(df_samp) <- sample_ids

 ps = phyloseq(
    otu_table(as.matrix(tab[,1:(ncol(tab)-6)]), taxa_are_rows=T),
    tax_table(as.matrix(tab[,(ncol(tab)-5):ncol(tab)])),
    sample_data(df_samp)
   )
 
# Remove MOCK samples and negative controls
samples_to_remove <- c("X136", "X137", "X138", "X139")
ps <- subset_samples(ps, !(sample_names(ps) %in% samples_to_remove))

# Reorder Factors
ps@sam_data[["Zone"]]      <- factor(ps@sam_data[["Zone"]], levels = c("PZ","LM","HM"))
ps@sam_data[["Treatment"]] <- recode_factor(ps@sam_data[["Treatment"]],  "0"="ambient", "1.5"="+1.5°C","3"="+3.0°C")
ps@sam_data[["Depth"]]     <- factor(ps@sam_data[["Depth"]],levels=c("0-5 cm","5-10 cm", "20-30 cm", "40-50 cm","80-100 cm" ))

# Estimat richness for different measures 
df_a_mean <- as.data.frame(estimate_richness(ps, measures=c("Observed", "Shannon","Chao1")))

# Add metadata to richness estimates
df_a_mean <- merge(df_samp, df_a_mean, by.x = "row.names", by.y = "row.names")

# Summarise dataframe for different alpha diversity measures
df_a_mean <- df_a_mean %>%
  group_by(Treatment, Zone, Depth) %>%
  summarise(mean_chao1 = mean(Chao1),
            se_chao1 = sd(Chao1) / sqrt(n()),
            mean_shannon = mean(Shannon),
            se_shannon = sd(Shannon) / sqrt(n()),
            mean_observed = mean(Observed),
            se_observed = sd(Observed) / sqrt(n()))

df_a_mean$Treatment  <- recode_factor(df_a_mean$Treatment ,  "0"="ambient", "1.5"="+1.5°C","3"="+3.0°C")
df_a_mean$Depth      <- factor(df_a_mean$Depth, levels = c("0-5 cm", "5-10 cm", "20-30 cm", "40-50 cm", "80-100 cm"))
df_a_mean$Depth_cont <- ifelse(df_a_mean$Depth == "0-5 cm", 2.5, 
                          ifelse(df_a_mean$Depth == "5-10 cm", 7.5, 
                          ifelse(df_a_mean$Depth == "20-30 cm", 25, 
                          ifelse(df_a_mean$Depth == "40-50 cm", 45, 90))))
df_a_mean$Zone       <- factor(df_a_mean$Zone, levels = c("PZ","LM","HM"))

# Alpha Diversity Plots (https://joey711.github.io/phyloseq/plot_richness-examples.html)
plot_a_all <-plot_richness(ps, "Zone","Treatment",  title="unmerged")+
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_palette, breaks = c("ambient", "+1.5°C", "+3.0°C"))+
    theme_JM

plot_a_T <- plot_richness(ps, x="Treatment", color="Treatment", measures=c("Observed", "Shannon","Chao1")) + 
    geom_boxplot()+
    scale_color_manual(values = my_palette, breaks = c("ambient", "+1.5°C", "+3.0°C"))+
    theme_JM

plot_a_Z <- plot_richness(ps, x="Zone", color="Zone", measures=c("Observed", "Shannon","Chao1")) + 
    geom_boxplot()+
    scale_color_manual(values = my_palette2, breaks = c("PZ", "LM", "HM"))+
    theme_JM

plot_a_TZ_Sha <- plot_richness(ps, x="Treatment", color="Treatment", measures=c("Shannon")) + 
    geom_boxplot()+
    scale_color_manual(values = my_palette, breaks = c("ambient", "+1.5°C", "+3.0°C"))+
    theme_JM+
    facet_wrap(~Zone, ncol=3)

plot_a_TZ_Cha <- plot_richness(ps, x="Treatment", color="Treatment", measures=c("Chao1")) + 
    geom_boxplot()+
    scale_color_manual(values = my_palette, breaks = c("ambient", "+1.5°C", "+3.0°C"))+
    theme_JM+
    facet_wrap(~Zone, ncol=3)

plot_a_TZD_Cha <- plot_richness(ps, x="Treatment", color="Treatment", measures=c("Chao1")) + 
    geom_boxplot()+
    scale_color_manual(values = my_palette, breaks = c("ambient", "+1.5°C", "+3.0°C"))+
    theme_JM+
    facet_wrap(~Zone*Depth, ncol=5)

# Plot the means with se as error bars
plot_a_D_Cha <-ggplot(df_a_mean, aes(x = Depth_cont, y = mean_chao1, color = Treatment)) +
  geom_point(size=3.5) + 
  coord_flip() +
  scale_x_reverse(name="Depth [cm]")+
  geom_line(size=1)+
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_chao1 - se_chao1, ymax = mean_chao1 + se_chao1, group="Depth"),alpha=1, size=.1,position = position_dodge(width = 22)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_palette, breaks = c("ambient", "+1.5°C", "+3.0°C")) +
  theme_JM +
  facet_wrap(~Zone, ncol = 5)

# Plot the means with se as error bars
plot_a_D_Sha <-ggplot(df_a_mean, aes(x = Depth_cont, y = mean_shannon, color = Treatment)) +
  geom_point(size=3.5) + 
  coord_flip() +
  scale_x_reverse(name="Depth [cm]")+
  geom_line(size=1)+
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_shannon - se_shannon, ymax = mean_shannon + se_shannon, group="Depth"),alpha=1, size=.1,position = position_dodge(width = 22)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_palette, breaks = c("ambient", "+1.5°C", "+3.0°C")) +
  theme_JM +
  facet_wrap(~Zone, ncol = 5)

plot_a_all
plot_a_T
plot_a_Z
plot_a_TZ_Sha
plot_a_TZ_Cha 
plot_a_TZD_Cha
plot_a_D_Cha 
plot_a_D_Sha
```

\pagebreak

# Heat maps
```{r,message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#Sort the OTUs by abundance and pick the top 20
top20OTU.names = names(sort(taxa_sums(ps), TRUE)[1:20])
#Cut down the physeq.tree data to only the top 10 Phyla
top20OTU = prune_taxa(top20OTU.names, ps)

plot_heatmap(top20OTU)

plot_heatmap(top20OTU, sample.label="Treatment", sample.order="Treatment", taxa.label="Genus", taxa.order="Phylum",low="white", high="purple", na.value="grey")

```
\pagebreak
# Beta Diversity
```{r,message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Transform phyloseq object to relative abundance (source: https://rpubs.com/lconteville/714853)
ps_beta = transform_sample_counts(ps, function(x) x / sum(x) * 100) 
head(otu_table(ps_beta)[,1:6])

rel_bray <- phyloseq::distance(ps_beta, method = "bray")
rel_bray <- as.matrix(rel_bray)

sub_dist <- list()
groups_zone <- sample_data(ps_beta)$Zone
groups_treatment <- sample_data(ps_beta)$Treatment

for (zone in levels(groups_zone)) { 
  for (treatment in levels(groups_treatment)) {
    row_group <- which(groups_zone == zone & groups_treatment == treatment)
    sample_group <- sample_names(ps_beta)[row_group]
    sub_dist[[paste(zone, treatment, sep = "_")]] <- rel_bray[sample_group, sample_group]
    sub_dist[[paste(zone, treatment, sep = "_")]][!lower.tri(sub_dist[[paste(zone, treatment, sep = "_")]])] <- NA
  }
}

braygroups <- melt(sub_dist)
df.bray <- braygroups[complete.cases(braygroups), ]
df.bray$Zone <- sapply(strsplit(as.character(df.bray$L1), "_"), `[`, 1)
df.bray$Treatment <- sapply(strsplit(as.character(df.bray$L1), "_"), `[`, 2)
df.bray$L1 <- NULL

# Plot beta diversity as boxplots
df.bray$Zone      <- factor(df.bray$Zone, levels = c("PZ","LM","HM"))
df.bray$Treatment <- recode_factor(df.bray$Treatment,  "0"="ambient", "1.5"="+1.5°C","3"="+3.0°C")

plot_beta<-ggplot(df.bray, aes(x=Zone, y=value, colour=Treatment)) +
  #geom_jitter(aes(x=Zone, y=value,color=Treatment)) + 
  geom_boxplot(alpha=0.6) +  
  theme(legend.position="none") +
  ylab("Bray-Curtis diversity") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#4575B4", "#FDAE61","#D73027"))+
  theme(axis.title.x=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=45,hjust=1,vjust=1,size=12), axis.text.y=element_text(size=12))+
  theme_JM


# Ordination
ord <- ordinate(ps_beta, method="PCoA", distance = "bray")

plot_ord_beta<-plot_ordination(ps_beta, ord, color = "Treatment", shape="Zone") + 
  geom_point(size=4) + 
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#4575B4", "#FDAE61","#D73027"))+
  stat_ellipse(aes(group=Zone))+
  theme_pubclean()

# ORdination alternative                                                      
#cap_ord <- ordinate(
 # physeq = ps_itree_ra, 
  #method = "CAP",
  #distance = bacs_dist,
  #formula = ~ pH + Ca + Mg + OM.percent+M3_P
#)

#generate distance matrices and NMDS ordinations
#set.seed(888)
#bacs_dist <- phyloseq::distance(ps_ra, "bray")#checks homogeneity of group dispersion
#check group dispersion
#sample_df <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_ra))
#beta <- betadisper(bacs_dist, sample_df$Treatment)
#permutest(beta)
#bacs.ord <- metaMDS(bacs_dist, distance = "bray", k = 2, trymax = 100)#stress 0.1113

#set.seed(888)

#PERMANOVA of Treatment
#adonis_sp<- adonis2(bacs_dist ~ Treatment*Zone, data=sample_df,  permutations = 999, method = "bray")
#adonis_sp#0.001
#pairwise.perm.manova(bacs_dist,sample_df$Treatment,nperm=100)
```
\pagebreak

# Step 7: Bar charts
## Step 7a: Absolute barcharts
```{r,message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

#Sort the Phyla by abundance and pick the top 10
top10P.names = sort(tapply(taxa_sums(ps), tax_table(ps)[, "Phylum"], sum), TRUE)[1:10]
#Cut down the phyloseq object to only the top 10 Phyla
top10P = subset_taxa(ps, Phylum %in% names(top10P.names))

# install speedyseq package to merge ps objects without transforming factors to double
remotes::install_github("mikemc/speedyseq")
library(speedyseq)

# Top 10 Phyla by Treatment
sample_data(top10P) <- sample_data(top10P) %>%
  transform(Treatment = Treatment)
sample_data(top10P) 

top10P_T_merged<-merge_samples2(top10P, "Treatment", fun_otu = mean, reorder = FALSE)
ps.phylum_T = tax_glom(top10P_T_merged, taxrank="Phylum", NArm=FALSE)

plot_bar_T <-plot_bar(ps.phylum_T,x="Treatment", fill="Phylum") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","#FDAE61","yellow","green3","darkgreen","purple","blue","#4575B4","grey","black"))+
theme_JM

# Top 10 Phyla by Zone
sample_data(top10P) <- sample_data(top10P) %>%
  transform(Zone = Zone)
sample_data(top10P) 

top10P_Z_merged<-merge_samples2(top10P, "Zone", fun_otu = mean, reorder = FALSE)
ps.phylum_Z = tax_glom(top10P_Z_merged, taxrank="Phylum", NArm=FALSE)

plot_bar_Z<- plot_bar(ps.phylum_Z,x="Zone", fill="Phylum") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","#FDAE61","yellow","green3","darkgreen","purple","blue","#4575B4","grey","black"))+
theme_JM

# Top 10 Phyla by Depth
sample_data(top10P) <- sample_data(top10P) %>%
  transform(Zone = Zone)
sample_data(top10P) 

top10P_D_merged<-merge_samples2(top10P, "Depth", fun_otu = mean, reorder = FALSE)
ps.phylum_D = tax_glom(top10P_D_merged, taxrank="Phylum", NArm=FALSE)

# Abundance Depth
plot_bar_D<-plot_bar(ps.phylum_D,x="Depth", fill="Phylum") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","#FDAE61","yellow","green3","darkgreen","purple","blue","#4575B4","grey","black"))+
theme_JM

# Relative Abundance Depth
ps.phylum_D_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps.phylum_D, function(x) 100 * x/sum(x))
plot_bar_D_rel<-plot_bar(ps.phylum_D_rel,x="Depth", fill="Phylum") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","#FDAE61","yellow","green3","darkgreen","purple","blue","#4575B4","grey","black"))+
theme_JM

# Top 10 Phyla by Treatment*Zone
sample_data(top10P) <- sample_data(top10P) %>%
  transform(Treatment_Zone = Treatment:Zone)
sample_data(top10P) 

top10P_TZ_merged<-merge_samples2(top10P, "Treatment_Zone", fun_otu = mean, reorder = FALSE)
ps.phylum_TZ = tax_glom(top10P_TZ_merged, taxrank="Phylum", NArm=FALSE)

# Abundance Treatment Zone
plot_bar_TZ<- plot_bar(ps.phylum_TZ,x="Treatment", fill="Phylum") + facet_wrap(~Zone, scales= "free_x", nrow=3)+  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","#FDAE61","yellow","green3","darkgreen","purple","blue","#4575B4","grey","black"))+
theme_JM

# Relative Abundance Treatment Zone
ps.phylum_TZ_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps.phylum_TZ, function(x) 100 * x/sum(x))
plot_bar_TZ_rel<- plot_bar(ps.phylum_TZ_rel,x="Treatment", fill="Phylum") + facet_wrap(~Zone, scales= "free_x", nrow=3)+  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","#FDAE61","yellow","green3","darkgreen","purple","blue","#4575B4","grey","black"))+
theme_JM

# Top 10 Phyla by Treatment*Zone* Sample
sample_data(top10P) <- sample_data(top10P) %>%
  transform(Zone_Plot = Zone:Plot)
sample_data(top10P) 

top10P_ZP_merged<-merge_samples2(top10P, "Zone_Plot", fun_otu = mean, reorder = FALSE)
ps.phylum_ZP = tax_glom(top10P_ZP_merged, taxrank="Phylum", NArm=FALSE)

ps.phylum_ZP_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps.phylum_ZP, function(x) 100 * x/sum(x))

# Abundance Plot
plot_bar_ZP<- plot_bar(ps.phylum_ZP,x="Plot", fill="Phylum") + facet_wrap(~Zone, scales= "free_x", nrow=3)+  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","#FDAE61","yellow","green3","darkgreen","purple","blue","#4575B4","grey","black"))+
theme_JM

# Realtive abundance Plot
ps.phylum_ZP_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps.phylum_ZP, function(x) 100 * x/sum(x))
plot_bar_ZP_rel<- plot_bar(ps.phylum_ZP_rel,x="Plot", fill="Phylum") + facet_wrap(~Zone, scales= "free_x", nrow=3)+  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","#FDAE61","yellow","green3","darkgreen","purple","blue","#4575B4","grey","black"))+
theme_JM

plot_bar_T
plot_bar_Z
plot_bar_D
plot_bar_D_rel
plot_bar_TZ
plot_bar_TZ_rel + theme(legend.position = "top")
plot_bar_ZP
plot_bar_ZP_rel
```
\pagebreak

## Step 7b: Relative barcharts (incl. others)
```{r,message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Alternative of showing Top9 Taxa and "others" + relative abundances
#https://github.com/joey711/phyloseq/issues/1197

### DECIDE how many taxa you want to see
topN = 10 # all others will be grouped in "Others"
topTaxa <- ps %>%  # CHOOSE YOUR DATASET !
  psmelt %>% group_by(Phylum) %>% # melt the table and group by tax level
  summarise(Abundance=sum(Abundance)) %>% # find most overall abundant taxa
  arrange(desc(Abundance)) %>% # order them 
  mutate(aggTaxo=as.factor(case_when( # aggTaxo will become the plot legend
    row_number()<=topN~Phylum, row_number()>topN~'Others'))) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-Abundance) %>% #flush abundance value
  head(n=topN+1) # +1 to include the Others section! 

### CREATE an object that will be fed into ggplot
ps_others <- ps %>% # CHOOSE YOUR DATASET !
  psmelt %>% # "melt" the phyloseq object into a table
  inner_join(.,topTaxa, by="Phylum") %>% # use topTaxa to join aggTaxo variable
  aggregate(Abundance~Treatment+aggTaxo+Zone, # Abundance is aggregated...
            data=., FUN=sum)  # ...sums "Others" taxa by specified variables

# Relative abundance counts plot
plot_others<-ggplot(ps_others, aes(x=Treatment, y=Abundance, fill=aggTaxo)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity",position="fill") + # change to "stack" to plot total counts
  labs( fill="Phylum", x="Zone", y="Relative abundance") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","#FDAE61","yellow","green3","darkgreen","purple","blue","#4575B4","grey","black", "white"))+
  facet_wrap(~Zone, nrow=3) + 
  theme_JM
  
plot_others
```


# Step 10: Tax4Fun2 - Visualize Functional Predictions
```{r}
my_colors <- c("darkblue","#4575B4", "yellow")
my_colors <- c("#efedf5","#bcbddc", "#756bb1")
my_colors <- c("#fee8c8","#fdbb84", "#e34a33")
#2ca25f
# Functional predictions
file_path_fun_pred <- "K:/PhD/data/WP2_microbial/GFZ_RNA_DNA/Tax4Fun2/Tax4Fun2_output_folder/functional_prediction_raw.txt"
df_fun_pred <- read_delim(file_path_fun_pred, delim = "\t", col_names = TRUE)

# All methanogenesis realted KOs
mg_ko_text <- "(K00200+K00201+K00202+K00203-K11261+(K00205,K11260,K00204)) K00672 K01499 (K00319,K13942) K00320 (K00577+K00578+K00579+K00580+K00581-K00582-K00583+ K00584) (K00399+K00401+K00402) (K22480+K22481+K22482,K03388+K03389+K03390,K08264+ K08265,K03388+K03389+K03390+K14127+(K14126+K14128,K22516+K00125)) ((K00925 K00625),K01895) (K00193+K00197+K00194) (K00577+K00578+K00579+K00580+ K00581-K00582-K00583+K00584) (K00399+K00401+K00402) (K22480+K22481+K22482,K03388+ K03389+K03390,K08264+K08265,K03388+K03389+K03390+K14127+(K14126+K14128,K22516+ K00125)) K14080+K04480+K14081 K00399+K00401+K00402 (K22480+K22481+K22482,K03388+ K03389+K03390,K08264+K08265,K03388+K03389+K03390+K14127+(K14126+K14128,K22516+ K00125)) K14082 ((K16177-K16176),(K16179-K16178),(K14084-K14083)) K00399+K00401+ K00402 (K22480+K22481+K22482,K03388+K03389+K03390,K08264+K08265,K03388+ K03389+K03390+K14127+(K14126+K14128,K22516+K00125))"
all_mg_ko_numbers <- unlist(regmatches(mg_ko_text, gregexpr("K\\d+", mg_ko_text)))
methanogenesis_ko <- unique(all_mg_ko_numbers)

# All methanotrophy realted KOs
methanotrophy_ko <- c("K10944", "K10945", "K10946", "K16157", "K16158", "K16159", "K16160","K16161", "K16162", "K14029", "K23995")

# All dissmiliatory sulfate reduction KOs
so_ko_text<- "(K00958 (K00394+K00395) (K11180+K11181-K27196) (K27187+K27188+K27189+K27190+K27191)"
all_so_ko_numbers <- unlist(regmatches(so_ko_text, gregexpr("K\\d+", so_ko_text)))
sulfate_reduction_ko <- unique(all_so_ko_numbers)

# All carbon fixiation in prokaryotes
c_ko_text <- "(K00169+K00170+K00171+K00172,K03737) (K01007,K01006) K01595,K01959+K01960,K01958) K00024 (K01676,K01679,K01677+K01678) (K00239+K00240-K00241-K00242K18556+K18557+K18558+K18559+K18560) (K01902+K01903) (K00174+K00175-K00177-K00176) K00031 (K01681,K01682) (K15230+K15231,K15232+K15233 K15234)(K02160+K01961+K01962+K01963) K14468 K14469 K15052 K05606 (K01847,K01848+K01849) (K14471+K14472) (K00239+K00240+K00241) K01679 K08691 K14449 K14470 K09709 (K01964+K15037+K15036) K15017 K15039 K15018 K15019 K15020 K05606 (K01848+K01849) (K15038,K15017) K14465 (K14466,K18861,K25774) K14534 K15016 K00626 	(K00169+K00170+K00171+K00172) K01007 K01595 K00024 (K01677+K01678) (K00239+K00240-K00241-K18860) (K01902+K01903) (K15038,K15017) K14465 (K14467,K18861,K25774) K14534 K15016 K00626 	K00198 (K05299-K15022,K22015+K25123+K25124) K01938 K01491-K01500 K00297-K25007-K25008 K15023 K14138+K00197+K00194 (K00625,K13788,K15024) K00925 (K00169+K00170+K00171+K00172) (K01959+K01960) K00024 (K01677+K01678) (K18209+K18210) (K01902+K01903) (K00174+K00175+K00176+K00177)"
all_c_ko_numbers <- unlist(regmatches(c_ko_text, gregexpr("K\\d+", c_ko_text)))
carbon_ko <- unique(all_c_ko_numbers)

  df_CH4 <- df_fun_pred %>%
  filter(KO %in% c(methanogenesis_ko, methanotrophy_ko, sulfate_reduction_ko, carbon_ko)) %>%
  mutate(pathway = case_when(
    KO %in% methanogenesis_ko ~ "methanogenesis",
    KO %in% methanotrophy_ko ~ "methanotrophy",
    KO %in% sulfate_reduction_ko ~ "sulfate reduction",
    KO %in% carbon_ko ~ "carbon fixation in prokaryotes",
    TRUE ~ "other"  # Optional: Assign "other" for KO numbers not matching any pathway
  ))


df_CH4_long <- reshape2::melt(df_CH4, id.vars = c("pathway", "KO", "description"))
df_CH4_long$value<-as.numeric(df_CH4_long$value)

df_CH4_long$sample_name <- df_CH4_long$variable
df_CH4_meta <- merge(df_CH4_long , metadata, by.x = "sample_name", by.y = "sample_name", all.x = TRUE)
df_CH4_meta$pathway<-as.factor(df_CH4_meta$pathway)
df_CH4_meta$value<-as.numeric(df_CH4_meta$value)

df_CH4_fac_sum <- df_CH4_meta%>%
   group_by(pathway, Treatment, Zone, Depth) %>%
  summarise(
    abundance = mean(value, na.rm=TRUE),
    se_abundance = sd(value, na.rm=TRUE) / sqrt(n())
  )%>%
  filter(!is.na(Treatment))

df_CH4_fac_sum$pathway    <- factor(df_CH4_fac_sum$pathway ,levels = c("carbon fixation in prokaryotes","methanotrophy", "sulfate reduction","methanogenesis"))
df_CH4_fac_sum$pathway    <- recode_factor(df_CH4_fac_sum$pathway ,  "carbon fixation in prokaryotes"="Carbon fixiation", "methanotrophy"="Methanotrophy","sulfate reduction"="Sulfate reduction", "methanogenesis"="Methanogenesis")
df_CH4_fac_sum$Treatment  <- recode_factor(df_CH4_fac_sum$Treatment ,  "0"="ambient", "1.5"="+1.5°C","3"="+3.0°C")
df_CH4_fac_sum$Zone       <- factor(df_CH4_fac_sum$Zone, levels = c("PZ","LM","HM"))
df_CH4_fac_sum$Depth      <- factor(df_CH4_fac_sum$Depth, levels = c("0-5 cm", "5-10 cm", "20-30 cm", "40-50 cm", "80-100 cm"))

plot_CH4 <- ggplot(df_CH4_fac_sum, aes(x = pathway, y =abundance, fill =Treatment)) +
   geom_bar(stat = "identity", position="dodge") + 
   geom_errorbar(aes(x=pathway,ymin = abundance - se_abundance, ymax = abundance + se_abundance, group=Treatment), width=0.4, colour="black", alpha=0.9, size=1,position = position_dodge(width = .9)) + 
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("ambient" = "#4575B4", "+1.5°C" = "#FDAE61", "+3.0°C" = "#D73027"), name = "Warming") +# Add error bars
   #labs(x = "Plot", y = "Redox potential [mV]", fill = "Plot") +
   theme_pubclean() +   
   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size = 16), axis.text.y =element_text(hjust = 1, vjust = 1,size=16))+
   labs(x = "Pathway", y = "Abundance")+
   facet_wrap(~Zone*Depth, ncol=5)

plot_heatmap_CH4<- ggplot(df_CH4_fac_sum, aes(x = Treatment, y = pathway, fill = abundance)) +
  geom_tile(color="grey") +
  labs(x = "Treatment", y = "Pathway", fill = "abundance") +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colors = my_colors, na.value = "white")  + 
  theme_pubclean() +  # Apply a theme (optional)
  facet_grid(~ Zone*Depth)+
  theme_pubclean()

plot_heatmap_CH4_Z<- ggplot(df_CH4_fac_sum, aes(x = Treatment, y = pathway, fill = abundance)) +
  geom_tile(color="grey") +
  labs(x = "Treatment", y = "Pathway", fill = "abundance") +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colors = my_colors, na.value = "white", name = "Abundance")  + 
  theme_pubclean() +  # Apply a theme (optional)
  facet_grid(~ Zone)  + 
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12, face = "bold"), 
        axis.text.x = element_text(colour = "black", face = "bold", size = 12), 
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12, face ="bold", colour ="black"), 
        legend.position = "right", axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14), 
        axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, colour = "black"), 
        legend.title = element_text(size = 14, colour = "black", face = "bold"), 
        panel.background = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, size = 1.2),
        legend.key=element_blank()) 
  #theme_pubclean()+
  #theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size = 14), axis.text.y =element_text(hjust = 1, vjust = 1,size=14),legend.title=element_text(size=14))

df_CH4_fac_sum_Z <- df_CH4_meta%>%
   group_by(pathway, Treatment, Zone) %>%
  summarise(
    abundance = mean(value, na.rm=TRUE),
    se_abundance = sd(value, na.rm=TRUE) / sqrt(n())
  )%>%
  filter(!is.na(Treatment))

df_CH4_fac_sum_Z$pathway    <- factor(df_CH4_fac_sum_Z$pathway ,levels = c("carbon fixation in prokaryotes","methanotrophy", "sulfate reduction","methanogenesis"))
df_CH4_fac_sum_Z$pathway    <- recode_factor(df_CH4_fac_sum_Z$pathway ,  "carbon fixation in prokaryotes"="Carbon fixiation", "methanotrophy"="Methanotrophy","sulfate reduction"="Sulfate reduction", "methanogenesis"="Methanogenesis")
df_CH4_fac_sum_Z$Treatment  <- recode_factor(df_CH4_fac_sum_Z$Treatment ,  "0"="ambient", "1.5"="+1.5°C","3"="+3.0°C")
df_CH4_fac_sum_Z$Zone       <- factor(df_CH4_fac_sum_Z$Zone, levels = c("PZ","LM","HM"))

plot_CH4_Z <- ggplot(df_CH4_fac_sum_Z, aes(x = pathway, y =abundance, fill =Treatment)) +
   geom_bar(stat = "identity", position="dodge") + 
   geom_errorbar(aes(x=pathway,ymin = abundance - se_abundance, ymax = abundance + se_abundance, group=Treatment), width=0.4, colour="black", alpha=0.9, size=1,position = position_dodge(width = .9)) + 
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("ambient" = "#4575B4", "+1.5°C" = "#FDAE61", "+3.0°C" = "#D73027"), name = "Warming") +# Add error bars
   #labs(x = "Plot", y = "Redox potential [mV]", fill = "Plot") +
   theme_pubclean() +
   facet_wrap(~Zone, ncol=3)+
   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size = 16), axis.text.y =element_text(hjust = 1, vjust = 1,size=16))+
  labs(x = "Pathway", y = "Abundance")

# Pathway predictions
file_path_fun_pred <- "K:/PhD/data/WP2_microbial/GFZ_RNA_DNA/MERIT_RNA_repo/pathway_prediction_raw.txt"
df_path_pred <- read_delim(file_path_fun_pred, delim = "\t", col_names = TRUE)

df_path_pred_long <- reshape2::melt(df_path_pred, id.vars = c("pathway", "level1", "level2", "level3"))
df_path_pred_long$value<-as.numeric(df_path_pred_long$value)

df_path_pred_long$sample_name <- df_path_pred_long$variable
df_path_pred_meta <- merge(df_path_pred_long , metadata, by.x = "sample_name", by.y = "sample_name", all.x = TRUE)
df_path_pred_meta$level1<-as.factor(df_path_pred_meta$level1)
df_path_pred_meta$value<-as.numeric(df_path_pred_meta$value)

df_path_pred_sum <- df_path_pred_meta%>%
   group_by(pathway, level1, level2, level3) %>%
  summarise(
    abundance = mean(value, na.rm=TRUE),
    se_abundance = sd(value, na.rm=TRUE) / sqrt(n())
  )

# Pathway predictions on factor level
df_path_pred_fac_sum <- df_path_pred_meta%>%
   group_by(pathway, level1, level2, level3, Treatment, Zone) %>%
  summarise(
    abundance = mean(value, na.rm=TRUE),
    se_abundance = sd(value, na.rm=TRUE) / sqrt(n())
  )

# Filter Top 10 Pathways
top_10_pathways <- df_path_pred_fac_sum %>%
  group_by(level2) %>%
  summarise(total_abundance = sum(abundance, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  top_n(10, total_abundance) %>%
  pull(level2)

# Filter the original dataframe to include only the top 10 pathways
df_path_pred_top10 <- df_path_pred_fac_sum %>%
  filter(level2 %in% top_10_pathways)%>%
  filter(!is.na(Treatment))

# Relative abundance
df_path_pred_top10 <- df_path_pred_top10 %>%
  group_by(Treatment, Zone)%>%
 mutate(abundance_norm = abundance / sum(abundance) * 100)

# Relative abundances with group for others
df_path_pred_others <- df_path_pred_fac_sum %>%
  filter(!level2 %in% top_10_pathways) %>%
  group_by(Treatment, Zone) %>%
  summarise(abundance = sum(abundance, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(level2 = "Others")
 

# Combine top 9 pathways with Others
df_path_pred_others_10 <- bind_rows(df_path_pred_top10, df_path_pred_others)

df_path_pred_others_10 <- df_path_pred_others_10%>%
  mutate(abundance_norm = abundance / sum(abundance) * 100)

# Adjust factors and reorder
df_path_pred_others_10$Treatment  <- recode_factor(df_path_pred_others_10$Treatment ,  "0"="ambient", "1.5"="+1.5°C","3"="+3.0°C")
df_path_pred_others_10$Zone      <- factor(df_path_pred_others_10$Zone, levels = c("PZ","LM","HM"))

# Create the stacked bar plot
plot_sbar_path_norm_others<- ggplot(df_path_pred_others_10, aes(fill = level2, y = abundance_norm, x = Treatment)) + 
  geom_bar(position = "stack", stat = "identity") +
  labs(x = "Treatment", y = "Abundance", fill = "Pathway") +  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c( "darkred","pink","#FDAE61","yellow","chartreuse2","darkgreen","purple","blue","#4575B4","grey","black"))+
  theme_pubclean()+
  facet_grid(~ Zone)
#  scale_fill_manual(values = c( "white","darkred","#D73027","pink","#FDAE61","yellow","green1","chartreuse3","darkgreen","purple","blue","#4575B4","cyan" ,"grey","black"))+

# Energy metabolism pathways
df_path_EMeta <- df_path_pred_fac_sum %>%
  filter(level2 == "Energy metabolism") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Treatment))  

# Adjust factors and reorder
df_path_EMeta$Treatment  <- recode_factor(df_path_EMeta$Treatment ,  "0"="ambient", "1.5"="+1.5°C","3"="+3.0°C")
df_path_EMeta$Zone       <- factor(df_path_EMeta$Zone, levels = c("PZ","LM","HM"))

# Heatmaps
my_colors <- c("#4575B4", "yellow", "#D73027")
plot_heatmap_EMeta <- ggplot(df_path_EMeta, aes(x = Treatment, y = level1, fill = abundance)) +
  geom_tile(color="grey") +
  labs(x = "Treatment", y = "Pathway", fill = "Abundance") +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colors = my_colors, na.value = "white")  + 
  theme_pubclean() +  # Apply a theme (optional)
  facet_grid(~ Zone)+
  theme_pubclean()

# Depth based analysis
# Pathway predictions on factor level
df_path_pred_facTZD_sum <- df_path_pred_meta%>%
   group_by(pathway, level1, level2, level3, Treatment, Zone, Depth) %>%
  summarise(
    abundance = mean(value, na.rm=TRUE),
    se_abundance = sd(value, na.rm=TRUE) / sqrt(n())
  )%>%
  mutate(abundance_norm = abundance / sum(abundance) * 100)

# Energy metabolism pathways Depth
df_path_TZD_EMeta <- df_path_pred_facTZD_sum%>%
  filter(level2 == "Energy metabolism") %>%
  #filter(!level1 == "Photosynthesis"& !level1 == "Photosynthesis - antenna proteins") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Treatment))  

# Adjust factors and reorder
df_path_TZD_EMeta$Treatment  <- recode_factor(df_path_TZD_EMeta$Treatment ,  "0"="ambient", "1.5"="+1.5°C","3"="+3.0°C")
df_path_TZD_EMeta$Zone       <- factor(df_path_TZD_EMeta$Zone, levels = c("PZ","LM","HM"))
df_path_TZD_EMeta$Depth      <- factor(df_path_TZD_EMeta$Depth, levels = c("0-5 cm", "5-10 cm", "20-30 cm", "40-50 cm", "80-100 cm"))

# Heatmaps
plot_heatmap_TZD_EMeta <- ggplot(df_path_TZD_EMeta, aes(x = Treatment, y = level1, fill = abundance)) +
  geom_tile(color="grey") +
  labs(x = "Treatment", y = "Pathway", fill = "Abundance") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = my_colors, na.value = "white")  + 
  theme_pubclean() + 
  facet_wrap(~ Zone*Depth, ncol = 5)+ 
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12, face = "bold"), 
        axis.text.x = element_text(colour = "black", face = "bold", size = 12), 
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12, face ="bold", colour ="black"), 
        legend.position = "right", axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14), 
        axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, colour = "black"), 
        legend.title = element_text(size = 14, colour = "black", face = "bold"), 
        panel.background = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, size = 1.2),
        legend.key=element_blank()) 

plot_CH4
plot_CH4_Z
plot_heatmap_CH4
plot_heatmap_CH4_Z
plot_heatmap
plot_heatmap_EMeta
plot_heatmap_TZD_EMeta
#plot_sbar_path
#plot_sbar_path_norm
plot_sbar_path_norm_others
```


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
